Welcome back to Exercise 4. This is called the Champagne Clink. This is when you could actually have a lot of fun with and do with an actual real client or success story. That would be the ideal if it's available to you and the whole premise is this. You want to celebrate their success with them. This is where you can send them wine or champagne in the mail or chocolate or tea or whatever feels appropriate for that client with this invitation to schedule a celebration call on Zoom. What you're going to do there is share that virtual champagne clink and really dive into their success and their story. What would they be sharing on that call? What was that starting point? What made them initially decide to pull the trigger and buy your product or service? What are they celebrating now? What is that outcome? What is that moment of highest pleasure that they're still beaming about and really opening up over with that champagne circulating through their body? What amazing results and highly detailed outcomes could they be sharing that would even surprise you as a creator? These could be some side benefits or residual outcomes that you wouldn't have even planned for but have now become available to them because of that one thing you solve, because of that moment of highest tension that no longer exists in their life. What new opportunities are available to them? What are they now pursuing based on this new end point in which you've left them? Once again, this could be done purely as a visualization if that's what we've got to go for for practical reasons or if your client is up for it, if you're up for it, if it's even available to you, you could take this to the next level and really make this part of your customer happiness or customer success plan where you will actually be sending them the physical champagne or wine or whatever they're into. Zoom call, have this call live, record it and then here's kind of where we make this more of a research thing. Get a transcribed, color code it so anything that talks about pain or moment of highest tension or past failures or motivations to join would be in orange, anything that has to do with success or wins or outcomes, the moment of highest pleasure, any emotional benefits would be in green and then things that would be kind of like initial points of hesitation, resistance and objections, those would be yellow and of course you can unpack that later when it comes to your marketing. So that is the champagne clink. That is how to turn customer success and celebrations into an extremely efficient and telling marketing research tool which ultimately allows you to coach the conversion at scale. So give that a go either in real life by sending champagne to someone who's really had success in your program or just take a few moments now, close your eyes and do this as more of a visual exercise and I will see you in the next one.
